 {"id":"frankenredis-dzg","title":"Architectural audit and performance optimization of multi-client state and Pub/Sub","description":"Audit and fix critical performance bottlenecks and architectural flaws: 1) Replace O(N) Pub/Sub pattern matching with cached results and inverse client mapping for cleanup. 2) Eliminate expensive ClientSession clones in swap_session by moving session storage to ServerState. 3) Fix O(N_blocked * N_keys) bottleneck in check_blocked_clients using a ready-list pattern. 4) Correct BLPOP behavior when keys already have data. 5) Mitigate LuaTable O(N) lookup DoS vector.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T02:21:26.822550544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T02:26:21.181195847Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T02:26:21.178753578Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-e0iq","title":"Add live Redis PFDEBUG parity harness and close behavior gaps","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:13:46.217121870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:48:40.800805760Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:48:40.798550593Z","close_reason":"Implemented PFDEBUG sparse/dense parity and live Redis conformance coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-e1e9","title":"Add persistence restart integration test (write → stop → restart → verify)","description":"# Persistence Restart Test

## What
An integration test that writes data to FrankenRedis with persistence enabled, gracefully stops the server, restarts it, and verifies all data survived. Tests BOTH AOF and RDB paths.

## Why
AOF round-trip is tested at the unit level (8 tests in aof_roundtrip_test.rs) but never through the full server lifecycle: TCP write → persist → shutdown → startup → reload → TCP read. The RDB path has encode/decode unit tests but no server-lifecycle test.

## Implementation

### Test A: AOF Restart
1. Start frankenredis with --aof /tmp/test_aof_file_$$
2. Via TCP: SET 50 keys with values, LPUSH list1 with 10 elements, HSET hash1 with 5 fields, ZADD zset1 with 5 members, XADD stream1 with 3 entries, XGROUP CREATE on stream1
3. Send SHUTDOWN NOSAVE (graceful exit without extra save — AOF already captured writes)
4. Restart frankenredis with same --aof path
5. Via TCP: verify all 50 keys, list contents, hash fields, zset scores, stream entries, consumer group exists
6. Verify DBSIZE matches expected count

### Test B: RDB Restart
1. Start frankenredis with --rdb /tmp/test_rdb_file_$$
2. Via TCP: SET 50 keys, same data structures as above
3. Send SAVE (force RDB snapshot)
4. Send SHUTDOWN NOSAVE
5. Restart frankenredis with same --rdb path
6. Via TCP: verify all data survived
7. Verify stream consumer groups survived (tests the new RDB consumer group persistence)

### Logging
- Log each write operation with key name
- Log verification result for each key (PASS/FAIL)
- Log file sizes (AOF bytes, RDB bytes) after write phase
- Log reload time on restart

## Files
- crates/fr-server/tests/persistence_restart_e2e.rs (new)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:21.255131142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:02:07.886573595Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:02:07.886287262Z","close_reason":"Added tcp_aof_restart_preserves_all_data and tcp_rdb_restart_preserves_all_data E2E tests. Also added Runtime::load_rdb() and RDB loading on server startup. All 11 fr-server tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-e3hr","title":"Implement LATENCY HISTOGRAM per-command tracking","description":"## What
LATENCY HISTOGRAM currently returns an empty array (stub). Redis returns cumulative distribution
data for per-command latencies when enabled.

## Location
crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12152-12158

## Current behavior
```rust
// LATENCY HISTOGRAM [command ...]
// Returns cumulative distribution of latencies for commands.
// Currently returns an empty array as per-command latency histograms
// are not yet tracked. Full implementation would return map-like data.
// TODO: Implement full histogram tracking per-command.
Ok(RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![])))
```

## Design
1. Add HdrHistogram or similar per-command latency tracking to Store or Runtime
2. Record command execution times in dispatch_argv return path
3. Return histogram bucket data in Redis-compatible format

## Acceptance criteria
- LATENCY HISTOGRAM returns actual per-command latency distributions
- Compatible with Redis LATENCY HISTOGRAM output format
- Minimal overhead when not actively querying histograms","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:03:01.317581137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T00:15:49.894705874Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T00:15:49.891747186Z","close_reason":"Implemented LATENCY HISTOGRAM per-command tracking with power-of-2 microsecond buckets, integrated latency recording at all command dispatch points","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
